feed.title: massivebugs
feed.subtitle: Write an awesome description for your new site here. You can edit this line in _config.yml. It will appear in your document head meta (for Google search results) and in your feed.xml site description.

entry.title: Running FFmpeg in a PHP script

link.title: Running FFmpeg in a PHP script

entry.content:
I spent a wasteful amount of time trying to use shell_exec() and retrieve the exit code, until I realized there was exec()

shell_exec() will return the output as a string, where as with exec(), you can get both the output and the exit code.

Here’s the code:

$output = [];
$result = 1;

exec('ffmpeg -i in.mp4 -hls_segment_filename \'segment%03d.ts\' out.m3u8', $output, $result)  

if($result === 0) {
    // Success: ...
} else {
    // Failure: report error and cleanup...
}
entry.summary: I spent a wasteful amount of time trying to use shell_exec() and retrieve the exit code, until I realized there was exec()

entry.title: Basic Docker commands I can never seem to recall…

link.title: Basic Docker commands I can never seem to recall…

entry.content:
• Pull an image or repo
    docker pull <name>

  • List all images
    docker images

  • List containers (-a for listing stopped containers too)
    docker ps -a

  • Run/start/stop a container process
    (-d for detached mode, -p for binding ports, –network for connecting to network, –name for setting container name) docker run -d -p<host_port>:<container_port> --network <network_name> --name <some_container_name> <image:version>
    docker start <container>
    docker stop <container>

  • Start/stop with Compose
    docker-compose -f <filename>.yaml up
    docker-compose -f <filename>.yaml down

  • Get container logs (-f/–follow to follow output like tail)
    docker logs -f <container>

  • Run bash shell in container
    docker exec -it <container> bash

  • Remove all containers/volumes/images
    docker rm -f $(docker ps -a -q)
    docker volume rm $(docker volume ls -q)
    docker rmi -f $(docker images -a -q

  • List, create networks
    (containers run with docker-compose are grouped to default network) docker network ls
    docker network create <some_network_name>
entry.summary: Pull an image or repo docker pull <name>
